briefdescription.para: Some objects support dynamic property creation at run-time.

detaileddescription.para: New properties are registered by calling RegisterProperty() with an unused property name. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Enumeration for Handle's capabilities that can be queried using Handle::Supports(). @SINCE_1_0.0.

memberdef.type: Signal< void(Handle handle, Property::Index index, const Property::Value &value)>
memberdef.definition: using Dali::Handle::PropertySetSignalType = Signal<void(Handle handle, Property::Index index, const Property::Value& value)>
memberdef.name: PropertySetSignalType

briefdescription.para: PropertySetSignal function prototype for signal handler. Called when a property is set on this object.
This constructor is used by Dali New() methods.
This constructor provides an uninitialized Dali::Handle.

detaileddescription.para:
This should be initialized with a Dali New() method before use. Methods called on an uninitialized Dali::Handle will assert. Handlehandle;//uninitialized handle.SomeMethod();//unsafe!Thiswillassert
handle=SomeClass::New();//nowinitialized handle.SomeMethod();//safe @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Dali::Handle is intended as a base class.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
A reference to the copied handle
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.
Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.22
rhs
A reference to the moved handle
A reference to this handle

briefdescription.para: Queries whether an handle supports a given capability.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
capability
The queried capability
True if the capability is supported

briefdescription.para: Queries how many properties are provided by an handle.

detaileddescription.para:
This may vary between instances of a class, if dynamic properties are supported. @SINCE_1_0.0
The number of properties

briefdescription.para: Queries the name of a property.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the property
The name of the property

briefdescription.para: Query the index of a property using the given key.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.27
key
The key of the property to search for. (The name or integer key provided to RegisterProperty()).
the matching property index of the key, or Property::INVALID_INDEX if no property matches the given key.

briefdescription.para: Queries whether a property can be set using SetProperty().

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the property
True if the property is writable
Property::INVALID_INDEX < index.

briefdescription.para: Queries whether a writable property can be the target of an animation or constraint.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the property
True if the property is animatable

briefdescription.para: Queries whether a property can be used as in input to a constraint.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the property
True if the property can be used as a constraint input

briefdescription.para: Queries the type of a property.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the property
The type of the property

briefdescription.para: Sets the value of an existing property.

detaileddescription.para:
Property should be write-able. Setting a read-only property is a no-op. @SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the property
propertyValue
The new value of the property
The property types match i.e. propertyValue.GetType() is equal to GetPropertyType(index).

briefdescription.para: Sets the value of an existing property by name.

detaileddescription.para: This is a convenience overload for looking up the property index from a string key before setting the value.

Property should be write-able. Setting a read-only property is a no-op. @SINCE_2_5.22
propertyName
The name of the property
propertyValue
The new value of the property
True if the property name was found and the set request was issued
The property types match i.e. propertyValue.GetType() is equal to GetPropertyType(index).

briefdescription.para: Reserves a number of custom properties.

detaileddescription.para: Saves automatic re-allocation of vectors for properties when we know in advance how many there will be.

parameterdescription.para: The total number of initial properties.

briefdescription.para: Registers a new animatable property.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
name
The name of the property
propertyValue
The new value of the property
The index of the property or Property::INVALID_INDEX if registration failed
The object supports dynamic properties i.e. Supports(Handle::DYNAMIC_PROPERTIES) returns true. Property names are expected to be unique, but this is not enforced. Property indices are unique to each registered custom property in a given object. returns Property::INVALID_INDEX if registration failed. This can happen if you try to register animatable property on an object that does not have scene graph object.
Only the following types can be animated:
Property::BOOLEAN
Property::FLOAT
Property::INTEGER
Property::VECTOR2
Property::VECTOR3
Property::VECTOR4
Property::MATRIX3
Property::MATRIX
Property::ROTATION
If a property with the desired name already exists, then the value given is just set.

briefdescription.para: Registers a new animatable property.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_1.6
name
The name of the property
propertyValue
The new value of the property
The index of the property or Property::INVALID_INDEX if registration failed
The object supports dynamic properties i.e. Supports(Handle::DYNAMIC_PROPERTIES) returns true. Property names are expected to be unique, but this is DEFINITELY not enforced. It is up to the caller to enforce uniqueness.
Property indices are unique to each registered custom property in a given object. returns Property::INVALID_INDEX if registration failed. This can happen if you try to register animatable property on an object that does not have scene graph object.
Only the following types can be animated:
Property::BOOLEAN
Property::FLOAT
Property::INTEGER
Property::VECTOR2
Property::VECTOR3
Property::VECTOR4
Property::MATRIX3
Property::MATRIX
Property::ROTATION
If a property with the desired name already exists, then this creates a secondary entry to a different scene graph property; Access by index works as expected, but uniform values will use the last registered version, not the existing version, so things may break.

briefdescription.para: Register a new animatable property with an integer key.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.27
key
The integer key of the property.
name
The text key of the property.
propertyValue
The new value of the property.
The index of the property or Property::INVALID_INDEX if registration failed
The object supports dynamic properties i.e. Supports(Handle::DYNAMIC_PROPERTIES) returns true. Property names and keys are expected to be unique, but this is not enforced. Property indices are unique to each registered custom property in a given object.
Todo
CHECK THIS!

Returns Property::INVALID_INDEX if registration failed. This can happen if you try to register animatable property on an object that does not have scene graph object.
The returned property index is not the same as the integer key (though it shares a type)
This version of RegisterProperty associates both an integer key and the text key with the property, allowing for lookup of the property index by either key or name ( which is useful when other classes know the key but not the name )

simplesect.para:
Only the following types can be animated:
Property::BOOLEAN
Property::FLOAT
Property::INTEGER
Property::VECTOR2
Property::VECTOR3
Property::VECTOR4
Property::MATRIX3
Property::MATRIX
Property::ROTATION
If a property with the desired name already exists, then the value given is just set.

briefdescription.para: Register a new animatable property with an integer key.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_1.6
key
The integer key of the property.
name
The text key of the property.
propertyValue
The new value of the property.
The index of the property or Property::INVALID_INDEX if registration failed It is up to the caller to guarantee that the property is unique. This allows many checks to be skipped.
The object supports dynamic properties i.e. Supports(Handle::DYNAMIC_PROPERTIES) returns true. Property names and keys are expected to be unique, and are therefore just added without any checks. Property indices are unique to each registered custom property in a given object.
Returns Property::INVALID_INDEX if registration failed. This can happen if you try to register animatable property on an object that does not have scene graph object.
The returned property index is not the same as the integer key (though it shares a type)
This version of RegisterProperty associates both an integer key and the text key with the property, allowing for lookup of the property index by either key or name ( which is useful when other classes know the key but not the name )

simplesect.para:
Only the following types can be animated:
Property::BOOLEAN
Property::FLOAT
Property::INTEGER
Property::VECTOR2
Property::VECTOR3
Property::VECTOR4
Property::MATRIX3
Property::MATRIX
Property::ROTATION

If a property with the desired name already exists, then this will create a second entry with the same name, and may cause problems. It is up to the caller to prevent this happening. Possible side effects are: lookup by name always finds the first such property, not the second; whereas, writing uniform value to shader will use the second, not the first; Using the returned Property::Index for future reference will always access the correct property.

briefdescription.para: Registers a new property.

detaileddescription.para:
Properties can be set as non animatable using property attributes. @SINCE_1_0.0
name
The name of the property
propertyValue
The new value of the property
accessMode
The property access mode (writable, animatable etc)
The index of the property
The handle supports dynamic properties i.e. Supports(Handle::DYNAMIC_PROPERTIES) returns true.
name is unused i.e. GetPropertyIndex(name) returns PropertyIndex::INVALID.
Only the following types can be animated:
Property::BOOLEAN
Property::FLOAT
Property::INTEGER
Property::VECTOR2
Property::VECTOR3
Property::VECTOR4
Property::MATRIX3
Property::MATRIX
Property::ROTATION
If a property with the desired name already exists, then the value given is just set.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves a property value.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the property
The property value
This returns the value set by SetProperty() or the animation target value if it is being animated.
To get the current value on the scene-graph, use GetCurrentProperty().

briefdescription.para: Convenience function for obtaining a property of a known type.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the property
The property value
The property types match i.e. PropertyTypes::Get<T>() is equal to GetPropertyType(index).
GetProperty()

briefdescription.para: Retrieves a property value by name.

detaileddescription.para: This is a convenience overload for looking up the property index from a string key before retrieving the value.

@SINCE_2_5.22
propertyName
The name of the property
The property value, or an empty Property::Value if the property name was not found
This returns the value set by SetProperty() or the animation target value if it is being animated.
To get the current value on the scene-graph, use GetCurrentProperty().

briefdescription.para: Convenience function for obtaining a property of a known type by name.

detaileddescription.para: This is a convenience overload for looking up the property index from a string key before retrieving the value.

@SINCE_2_5.22
propertyName
The name of the property
The property value, or a default-constructed T if the property name was not found
The property types match i.e. PropertyTypes::Get<T>() is equal to GetPropertyType(index).
GetProperty(const Dali::String&)

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the latest value of the property from the scene-graph.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.41
index
The index of the property
The property value
This returns the value of the property in the last rendered frame so can be different to that set by SetProperty() if the set-message has not been processed by the scene-graph yet.
To retrieve the value set by SetProperty(), use GetProperty().

briefdescription.para: Convenience function for obtaining the current value of a property of a known type.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.41
index
The index of the property
The property value
The property types match i.e. PropertyTypes::Get<T>() is equal to GetPropertyType(index).
GetCurrentProperty()

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the latest value of the property from the scene-graph by name.

detaileddescription.para: This is a convenience overload for looking up the property index from a string key before retrieving the current value.

@SINCE_2_5.22
propertyName
The name of the property
The property value, or an empty Property::Value if the property name was not found
This returns the value of the property in the last rendered frame so can be different to that set by SetProperty() if the set-message has not been processed by the scene-graph yet.
To retrieve the value set by SetProperty(), use GetProperty().

briefdescription.para: Convenience function for obtaining the current value of a property of a known type by name.

detaileddescription.para: This is a convenience overload for looking up the property index from a string key before retrieving the current value.

@SINCE_2_5.22
propertyName
The name of the property
The property value, or a default-constructed T if the property name was not found
The property types match i.e. PropertyTypes::Get<T>() is equal to GetPropertyType(index).
GetCurrentProperty(const Dali::String&)

briefdescription.para: Sets all the properties in the given property map.
Retrieves all the properties and the values for this object.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.27
properties
A map which is populated with the index-value pairs
The properties map will be cleared by this method first.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves all the property indices for this object (including custom properties).

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
indices
A container of property indices for this object
The added container is cleared.

briefdescription.para: Determine if the custom property index exists on this object without throwing a Dali::Exception.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.27 
This does not check default properties. 



index



The index of the property to test for

briefdescription.para: Adds a property notification to this object.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the property
condition
The notification will be triggered when this condition is satisfied
A handle to the newly created PropertyNotification

briefdescription.para: Adds a property notification to this object.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the property
componentIndex
Index to the component of a complex property such as a Vector
condition
The notification will be triggered when this condition is satisfied
A handle to the newly created PropertyNotification

briefdescription.para: Removes a property notification from this object.
Removes all property notifications from this object. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Removes all default tag constraints and custom constraints from an Object.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The object has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Removes all the constraint from the Object with a matching tag.

detaileddescription.para:
We can only use tag between [ConstraintTagRanges::CUSTOM_CONSTRAINT_TAG_START ConstraintTagRanges::CUSTOM_CONSTRAINT_TAG_END] or ConstraintTagRanges::DEFAULT_TAG
@SINCE_1_0.0
tag
The tag of the constraints which will be removed
The Object has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Removes all the constraint from the Object with a range of tags [tagBegin tagEnd).

detaileddescription.para:
We can only use tag between [ConstraintTagRanges::CUSTOM_CONSTRAINT_TAG_START ConstraintTagRanges::CUSTOM_CONSTRAINT_TAG_END] or ConstraintTagRanges::DEFAULT_TAG
@SINCE_2_4.14
tagBegin
The begin tag of the constraints which will be removed (include)
tagEnd
The end tag of the constraints which will be removed (exclusived)
The Object has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Index operator, using integer lookup.

detaileddescription.para: Returns an object that can be assigned to or cast from, enabling the indexed property to be either read or written.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the property to access.

simplesect.para: indirect value. Should have shorter scope than the handle

briefdescription.para: Index operator, using name lookup.

detaileddescription.para: Returns an object that can be assigned to or cast from, enabling the named property to be either read or written.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the property to access.

simplesect.para: indirect value. Should have shorter scope than the handle

briefdescription.para: Get a signal when a property is set on this object through the API (i.e. not when animating)

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.27
The signal to attach a connection to.

briefdescription.para: Creates a new object.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
A handle to a newly allocated object

briefdescription.para: Template to create a derived handle and set properties on it.

detaileddescription.para:
Marked as DALI_NO_EXPORT_API to prevent internal usage exporting symbols. @SINCE_1_9.27
T
The derived class to create
properties
The properties to set

briefdescription.para: Downcasts to a handle.

detaileddescription.para:
If not, the returned handle is left uninitialized. @SINCE_1_0.0
handle
to An object
handle or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: Dali::Handle is a handle to an internal property owning Dali object that can have constraints applied to it. @SINCE_1_0.0.
